summary: Define calculator math API, function signatures, and shared types for all operations.
description: Create the public contract for the calculator: function names, parameter types (e.g. numbers or big decimals), return types, and any shared types or enums (e.g. operation kind). Document expected behavior and error semantics so implementers and tests stay aligned.
objective: Establish a clear, typed API and shared types so all calculator operations and callers use a consistent interface.
requirements: Public function signatures for add, subtract, multiply, divide
Shared types for operands and result (e.g. number or decimal)
Documentation of parameter order and return shape
constraints: API must be language- and runtime-appropriate for the codebase
No implementation logic in this task; types/signatures only

summary: Implement add, subtract, multiply, and divide with correct numeric behavior.
description: Implement the four basic arithmetic operations using the agreed API. Use the language’s standard numeric types or chosen decimal type. Ensure correct handling of signs and integer vs floating-point behavior as specified in the API.
objective: Deliver working add, subtract, multiply, and divide that match the defined API and behave correctly for normal inputs.
requirements: Implement add with correct numeric semantics
Implement subtract with correct numeric semantics
Implement multiply with correct numeric semantics
Implement divide with correct numeric semantics
constraints: Must conform to the API and types defined in calculator-api-types
No UI or I/O in this task; pure math functions only

summary: Add error handling for invalid inputs and edge cases (e.g. division by zero).
description: Handle invalid or unsafe cases: division by zero, non-numeric or out-of-range inputs if applicable, and any other edge cases documented in the API. Define whether errors are thrown, returned as results, or logged, and keep behavior consistent across operations.
objective: Make the calculator safe and predictable for invalid or edge-case inputs without crashing or returning incorrect results.
requirements: Division by zero is handled and does not crash or return incorrect value
Invalid or unsupported inputs are handled in a defined way
Error behavior is documented and consistent
constraints: Error strategy (exceptions vs result types) must match project conventions
No silent wrong results; failures must be explicit

summary: Add unit tests for all calculator operations and error cases.
description: Write automated tests for add, subtract, multiply, and divide: normal cases, zero, negatives, and boundary values. Include tests for error handling (e.g. division by zero, invalid input). Tests must be runnable via the project’s test runner and stay green.
objective: Verify correctness and error behavior of the calculator and prevent regressions.
requirements: Tests for each operation with typical and edge inputs
Tests for defined error cases (e.g. division by zero)
Tests executable by the project’s test command and passing
constraints: Use the project’s existing test framework and conventions
No production logic in test files; tests only
